questions.text: What is the meaning of the word 'Al-Furqan' and to whom was it revealed?
questions.answer: It means 'The Criterion' (to judge between right and wrong), and it was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (SAW) so he may be a warner to the worlds.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His dominion in the opening of the Surah?
questions.answer: To Him belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He has not taken a son and has no partner in dominion, and He created all things and determined them with precise determination.

questions.text: What was the disbelievers' accusation against the Quran?
questions.answer: They said, 'This is nothing but a lie he has invented and others have helped him with it,' and they called it 'legends of the former peoples' which were dictated to him.

questions.text: How does Allah respond to the claim that the Quran is dictated?
questions.answer: He says: 'It has been sent down by Him who knows the secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful.'

questions.text: How did the disbelievers mock the human nature of the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: They said: 'What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel or a treasure, or why does he not have a garden to eat from?'

questions.text: What did the wrongdoers call the Prophet when they rejected him?
questions.answer: They said, 'You follow not but a man affected by magic.'

questions.text: What is the fate of those who deny the Hour (Day of Judgment)?
questions.answer: Allah has prepared for them a Blaze (Hell).

questions.text: How does Hell react when it sees the disbelievers from a distance?
questions.answer: They will hear its raging and its roaring.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers cry out for when they are thrown into a narrow place in the Fire?
questions.answer: They will cry out for destruction (death), but they will be told: 'Do not cry this day for one destruction but cry for many destructions.'

questions.text: What is the 'Garden of Eternity' (Jannat al-Khuld) promised to the righteous?
questions.answer: It is their reward and destination, where they will have whatever they wish, abiding therein forever.

questions.text: What will Allah ask the false gods on the Day of Judgment regarding their worshippers?
questions.answer: He will ask: 'Was it you who misled these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?'

questions.text: What will be the response of the false gods to Allah's question?
questions.answer: They will say: 'Exalted are You! It was not for us to take any allies besides You. But You provided for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined.'

questions.text: Were the messengers sent before Muhammad (SAW) also human?
questions.answer: Yes, 'We did not send before you any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets.'

questions.text: What was the demand of those who did not expect the meeting with Allah?
questions.answer: They said, 'Why were the angels not sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?'

questions.text: Will the disbelievers be happy when they finally see the angels on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, on that Day there will be no glad tidings for the criminals, and the angels will say, '[Joy is] forbidden and prevented!'

questions.text: What will happen to the deeds of the disbelievers on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Allah will regard whatever deeds they did and make them like dispersed dust (Haba'an Manthura).

questions.text: What will be the state of the people of Paradise on that Day?
questions.answer: They will have the best settlement and the finest resting place.

questions.text: What will happen to the heaven on the Day of Judgment according to this Surah?
questions.answer: The heaven will split open with clouds and the angels will be sent down in succession.

questions.text: To whom belongs the true sovereignty on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: To the Most Merciful (Ar-Rahman), and it will be a difficult day for the disbelievers.

questions.text: What will the wrongdoer do out of regret on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: He will bite on his hands, saying: 'Oh, I wish I had taken a path with the Messenger'.

questions.text: What is the regret regarding 'friendship' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: 'Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend. He misled me from the message after it had come to me.'

questions.text: What complaint will the Messenger make against his people on the Last Day?
questions.answer: 'O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Quran as something abandoned (Mahjura).'

questions.text: Who are the 'enemies' every prophet had to face?
questions.answer: Criminals from among their own people; but Allah is sufficient as a Guide and Helper.

questions.text: What was the disbelievers' criticism of the Quran being revealed gradually?
questions.answer: They asked: 'Why was the Quran not sent down to him all at once?'

questions.text: What was Allah's reason for revealing the Quran in stages?
questions.answer: To strengthen the heart of the Prophet (SAW) and to explain it in a better way (orderly and clearly).

questions.text: Who are those who will be 'dragged on their faces' to Hell?
questions.answer: Those who are in the worst place and the most astray from the path.

questions.text: Briefly mention the people of Musa and the punishment of those who denied them.
questions.answer: Allah gave Musa the Scripture and made Harun his helper; those who denied them were destroyed utterly.

questions.text: Who were the 'People of the Well' (Ashab al-Rass)?
questions.answer: A nation mentioned along with 'Ad and Thamud who were destroyed for their disbelief.

questions.text: Did the disbelievers learn from the 'town of evil rain' (the people of Lut)?
questions.answer: No, even though they passed by it, they did not expect resurrection and remained in denial.

questions.text: How does Allah describe someone who takes his 'desire' as his god?
questions.answer: As someone whom the Prophet cannot be a guardian over; they are like cattle, or even more astray from the path.

questions.text: What is the shadow's purpose mentioned as a sign of Allah's power?
questions.answer: Allah extends it and could have made it still, but He made the sun its guide, then He withdraws it to Himself gradually.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the night and the day in terms of rest and movement?
questions.answer: He made the night as a clothing, sleep for rest, and the day for rising (living).

questions.text: What is the 'Pure Water' (Ma'an Tahura) sent from the sky for?
questions.answer: To give life to dead land and to provide drink for animals and many people.

questions.text: What is the 'barrier' between the two seas mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: An invisible partition (Barzakh) between fresh, sweet water and salty, bitter water, keeping them from mixing.

questions.text: From what did Allah create every human being according to this Surah?
questions.answer: From water, then made for him blood relationship and relationship by marriage.

questions.text: What is the response of the disbelievers when told to prostrate to the Most Merciful (Ar-Rahman)?
questions.answer: They say, 'And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?' and it increases them in aversion.

questions.text: What celestial features has Allah placed in the sky?
questions.answer: Constellations (Buruj), a great lamp (the sun), and a glowing moon.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the alternation of the night and the day according to this section?
questions.answer: For whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.

questions.text: Who are the 'Ibadur Rahman'?
questions.answer: The 'Servants of the Most Merciful' whose qualities are described at the end of the Surah.

questions.text: How do the 'Ibadur Rahman' walk upon the earth?
questions.answer: They walk with humility and ease (Hawnan).

questions.text: How do the 'Ibadur Rahman' respond when the ignorant address them?
questions.answer: They respond by saying: 'Salam' (Peace).

questions.text: How do the servants of the Merciful spend their nights?
questions.answer: Prostrating and standing in prayer before their Lord.

questions.text: What is the prayer of the 'Ibadur Rahman' regarding Hell?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering (Gharama).'

questions.text: What is the attitude of the 'Ibadur Rahman' toward spending?
questions.answer: They are neither extravagant nor stingy, but maintain a just balance between the two.

questions.text: Which three major sins are mentioned that the servants of Allah avoid?
questions.answer: Associating partners with Allah (Shirk), killing a soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, and committing adultery/fornication (Zina).

questions.text: What happens to the punishment of those who commit these major sins?
questions.answer: It will be multiplied for them on the Day of Resurrection, and they will abide in it humiliated.

questions.text: What is the exception for those who have committed such sins?
questions.answer: Those who repent, believe, and do righteous work; for them, Allah will replace their evil deeds with good ones.

questions.text: What is the quality of the 'Ibadur Rahman' regarding false testimony?
questions.answer: They do not bear witness to falsehood, and when they pass by ill speech, they pass by with dignity.

questions.text: How do they react when reminded of the verses of their Lord?
questions.answer: They do not fall upon them deaf and blind, but listen and understand.

questions.text: What is the prayer of the 'Ibadur Rahman' for their families?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous (Imaman lil-Muttaqin).'

questions.text: What is the reward for the servants of the Most Merciful for their patience?
questions.answer: They will be awarded the high chamber (Paradise) and greeted therein with 'Salam'.

questions.text: Why does Allah say He wouldn't care for people if not for their 'Dua' (supplication)?
questions.answer: Because worship and calling upon Him is the primary purpose of their existence; without it, they have no value to Him.

questions.text: What is the 'High Chamber' (Al-Ghurfah)?
questions.answer: A noble and high station in Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'Dispersed Dust' (Haba'an Manthura)?
questions.answer: The worthlessness of the deeds of those who reject faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Lamp' (Siraj) in the sky?
questions.answer: The sun.

questions.text: What is the 'Barrier' (Hijran Mahjura)?
questions.answer: The natural separation between bodies of water that do not mix.

questions.text: What is the 'Relationship by marriage' (Sihra)?
questions.answer: The social bonds created through the union of families.

questions.text: What is the 'Abandoned Quran' (Mahjura)?
questions.answer: Neglecting its recitation, understanding, and application.

questions.text: What is the 'Saihah' mentioned regarding past nations?
questions.answer: A powerful divine shriek or sound used for punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Jihad' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Striving with the Quran against disbelief and falsehood.

questions.text: What is the 'Resting place' (Maqila)?
questions.answer: A place of peace and comfort in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Shadow' (Zill) a metaphor for?
questions.answer: The temporary nature of life and Allah's control over it.

questions.text: What is the 'Gharama' punishment?
questions.answer: A continuous and inescapable suffering in Hell.

questions.text: What is 'Extravagance' (Israf)?
questions.answer: Spending beyond necessity and in sinful ways.

questions.text: What is 'Stinginess' (Iqtar)?
questions.answer: Withholding wealth from where it is due.

questions.text: What is the 'Example for the righteous' (Imaman lil-Muttaqin)?
questions.answer: A role model of faith and character for others to follow.

questions.text: What is the 'Dignity' (Karama) of the servants of Allah?
questions.answer: Passing by evil or useless things without getting involved or debasing themselves.

questions.text: What is the 'Comfort of the eyes' (Qurrata A'yun)?
questions.answer: The joy and peace one feels seeing their family on the straight path.

questions.text: What is the 'Criterion' revealed for?
questions.answer: To warn all of mankind.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the 'walking in markets' of prophets?
questions.answer: To emphasize that they were real, relatable human beings with normal lives.

questions.text: What is the 'Evil Rain'?
questions.answer: The stones that rained down upon the people of Sodom.

questions.text: What is the 'Raging' of the Fire?
questions.answer: The intense sound and fury of Hell.

questions.text: What is the 'Shadow' guided by the sun?
questions.answer: A sign of how Allah manages the physical world for us.

questions.text: What is the 'Clothing' of the night?
questions.answer: The darkness that covers everything, providing privacy and rest.

questions.text: What is the 'Resurrection' (Nushura)?
questions.answer: Being raised up for judgment after death.

questions.text: What is the 'Salty and Bitter' water?
questions.answer: The water of the oceans, made distinct from the rivers.

questions.text: What is the 'Blood relationship' (Nasaba)?
questions.answer: Kinship through birth and direct lineage.

questions.text: What is the 'Secret' known to Allah?
questions.answer: Everything hidden in the universe and in human hearts.

questions.text: What is the 'Warner' (Nadhira) role of the Quran?
questions.answer: To alert people to the consequences of rejecting the Truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Forgiving and Merciful' nature of Allah?
questions.answer: His readiness to accept repentance and His kindness toward His creation.
